intent: Refactor the Courses page control bar from an undifferentiated row of widgets into clearly grouped, labeled sections (Filter, Sort, View). Improve card hover/focus states, increase metadata contrast, sharpen metadata density per view mode, add filter summary chip, and rename section heading from 'Imported Courses' to 'Your Courses'.
reviewers: julik-frontend-races (UI interactive state, DOM events, animations)

findings.title: File size text uses text-muted-foreground/70 reducing contrast below WCAG AA
findings.why_it_matters: The file size text uses text-muted-foreground/70 which is 70% opacity of an already potentially low-contrast token. This fails WCAG AA 4.5:1 contrast for the secondary metadata text, directly contradicting R5 which requires WCAG AA contrast on dark backgrounds.
findings.suggested_fix: Change text-muted-foreground/70 to text-muted-foreground (matching sibling elements) or adjust via the theme token approach described in Unit 4.
evidence: Line 703: className="text-muted-foreground/70" on file size span
Sibling elements (video count, PDF count) use className="text-muted-foreground" without opacity
R5 requires WCAG AA contrast for secondary metadata

findings.title: hover:scale-[1.01] may cause text blurriness on some browsers
findings.why_it_matters: Using transform: scale() on a card containing text, images, and gradient backgrounds can cause sub-pixel rendering artifacts. Text may appear slightly blurry when rendered at non-integer pixel positions after scaling, especially on non-Retina displays. The plan risk table acknowledges this ("Hover effects may cause layout shift in grid") and correctly recommends GPU-composited transforms, but scale can still degrade text quality.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace hover:scale-[1.01] with hover:-translate-y-0.5 hover:shadow-md to achieve a lift effect without text scaling artifacts. Keep motion-safe/motion-reduce guards.
evidence: Line 303: hover:scale-[1.01] on the article wrapper
Text and gradient backgrounds inside the card will be scaled
translateY achieves similar visual lift without rasterization artifacts

findings.title: Filter summary chip shows raw status keys instead of human-readable labels
findings.why_it_matters: The summary chip uses selectedStatuses.join(', ') which renders internal keys like "not-started, active" instead of human-readable labels like "Not Started, Active". Users will see technical identifiers rather than friendly status names, creating a poor UX and potential confusion.
findings.suggested_fix: Create a mapping from status keys to labels (reusing the existing statuses array in StatusFilter.tsx or defining a local const map) and join the display labels instead: selectedStatuses.map(s => statusLabels[s] ?? s).join(', ')
evidence: Line 274: Filtered by: {selectedStatuses.join(', ')}
Status keys are kebab-case internal identifiers (not-started, active, completed, paused)
StatusFilter.tsx defines human-readable labels: 'Not Started', 'Active', 'Completed', 'Paused'

findings.title: GridColumnControl active state uses bg-brand-soft while adjacent ViewModeToggle uses bg-brand
findings.why_it_matters: GridColumnControl and ViewModeToggle sit side by side in the View section but use different active state styles. ViewModeToggle uses bg-brand (strong fill) per Unit 3, while GridColumnControl retains bg-brand-soft (subtle fill). This creates visual inconsistency within the same toolbar section, making the column control look like a different control family.
findings.suggested_fix: Consider whether GridColumnControl should also use bg-brand for active state to match ViewModeToggle, or document the intentional difference if the subordinate control warrants a lower visual weight.
evidence: GridColumnControl line 70: data-[state=on]:bg-brand-soft data-[state=on]:text-brand-soft-foreground
ViewModeToggle line 57: data-[state=on]:bg-brand data-[state=on]:text-brand-foreground
Both controls sit inside the same ControlBarSection label='View' group

findings.title: Compact card hover ring may lack contrast on dark backgrounds
findings.why_it_matters: The compact card hover effect uses hover:ring-1 hover:ring-border. The border token is typically a subtle color by design (~hsl(var(--border))), which may not provide sufficient visible affordance on dark backgrounds where the card edge already blends. Compact cards have limited space, so the hover state needs to be clearly perceptible.
findings.suggested_fix: Consider hover:ring-1 hover:ring-muted-foreground/30 or hover:shadow-sm for a more visible hover affordance. Verify contrast in dark mode.
evidence: Line 209: hover:ring-1 hover:ring-border
Plan requires R6: clear hover/focus states with visible affordance
border token is intentionally subtle and may not provide sufficient hover distinction

findings.title: Test uses brittle className string assertions instead of toHaveClass()
findings.why_it_matters: The test at lines 49-51 uses expect(label.className).toContain('uppercase') which compares against the raw className string. This is brittle — if Tailwind reorders classes or additional classes are added, the assertion may break or give unclear failure messages. jest-dom's toHaveClass() is the idiomatic and more resilient matcher.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace expect(label.className).toContain(...) with expect(label).toHaveClass(...) from @testing-library/jest-dom, which handles class ordering and partial matches gracefully.
evidence: Lines 49-51: expect(label.className).toContain('uppercase'), .toContain('tracking-wider'), .toContain('text-xs')
jest-dom's toHaveClass() is the standard matcher for class assertions

findings.title: ControlBarSection divider coupling to instance ordering creates maintainability trap
findings.why_it_matters: The component's JSDoc tells callers to use showDivider={false} on the first section and showDivider={true} (default) on subsequent sections. This couples correctness to instance ordering. If sections are reordered in Courses.tsx, the divider logic must be manually updated. A more robust approach would be to have the parent control dividers, or to detect first-child position automatically.
findings.suggested_fix: Either: (a) have Courses.tsx manage dividers explicitly between sections, (b) use CSS :first-child to suppress the first divider automatically, or (c) keep as-is since the three-section layout is stable and unlikely to be reordered.
evidence: JSDoc lines 17-18: 'The first section should use showDivider={false}; subsequent sections use the default showDivider={true}'
Plan's Implementation Notes already flags this as a 'Low' concern
Three sections (Filter, Sort, View) are logically stable in this order

R1.notes: ControlBarSection wrapper created with labeled groups, dividers, and consistent spacing. Three groups (Filter, Sort, View) implemented in Courses.tsx.

R2.notes: ViewModeToggle active state strengthened to bg-brand with ring-1 and focus-visible ring. Selected state now highly visible.

R3.notes: GridColumnControl wrapped inside View section alongside ViewModeToggle, conditionally rendered only in grid mode via {courseViewMode === 'grid' && ...}.

R4.notes: Compact cards: metadata is minimal (thumbnail+title+progress) per plan. Grid cards: metadata layout improved with mt-auto, icons increased to size-4. List rows: metadata reordered above progress bar for scannable order. However, contrast on file size element uses text-muted-foreground/70 which fails R4's 'high contrast' requirement.

R5.notes: No theme token adjustments made. The pre-existing text-muted-foreground/70 on file size (ImportedCourseCard.tsx:703) remains and fails WCAG AA. Unit 4 deferred token-level changes or per-component overrides.

R6.notes: Grid cards: hover:shadow-md hover:scale-[1.01] with motion-safe/reduce guards. Compact cards: hover:ring-1 hover:ring-border with transition-shadow. Both cards have focus-visible rings.

R7.notes: Section heading renamed from 'Imported Courses' to 'Your Courses' at line 289 of Courses.tsx.

residual_risks: The scale transform (hover:scale-[1.01]) on ImportedCourseCard may cause text blurriness; consider translateY as alternative
Filter summary chip shows raw kebab-case status keys instead of human-readable labels
GridColumnControl active state differs from adjacent ViewModeToggle (brand-soft vs brand)
testing_gaps: No test for filter summary chip visibility when statuses are selected
No test for 'Clear all' button in filter summary
No test verifying human-readable labels in filter summary chip
No test for GridColumnControl hiding in non-grid view modes (may be covered by existing tests)
learnings: The Separator component's data-slot and data-orientation attributes are the correct selectors for testing separator presence and orientation
ControlBarSection is a ~30-line abstraction that pays for itself in layout consistency
